subject: science

primary.justification: The chapter's central domain is natural resources and their responsible use. Every section — air, water, solar energy, forests, soil, rocks, minerals, fossil fuels — builds toward the chapter's closing theme of judicious consumption of resources and mitigation of pollution. C-5.1 explicitly names 'judicious consumption of resources' and 'mitigation of climate change' as its scope, which is the precise cognitive object the chapter develops: students learn to classify resources as renewable or non-renewable and evaluate conservation choices. No other CG-1 through CG-4 domain matches this organising logic — the chapter does not primarily investigate matter properties (CG-1), physical forces (CG-2), living organisms (CG-3), or health (CG-4). The multi-resource survey structure and the renewable/non-renewable framework are architectural expressions of C-5.1.

dissolution_test: This chapter builds the student's ability to classify and evaluate natural resources by tracing their properties, replenishment rates, and societal dependencies in order to develop informed judgements about responsible consumption.
